feed.title: Apple pays $12.1 mln fine for alleged app market abuse in Russia - Antimonopoly Service
feed.summary: Apple has paid a 906 million rouble ($12.12 million) fine to Russia's Federal Antimonopoly Service (FAS) over alleged abuse of its dominant position in the mobile apps market. The FAS had ruled in August 2020 that Apple's control over app distribution via its iOS operating system gave its own products a competitive advantage. This settlement follows a previous complaint from cybersecurity company Kaspersky Lab and comes after Apple paused product sales in Russia a year ago due to the conflict in Ukraine.

feed.title: Apple iPhone's Clean Energy Charging causes stir on social media over battery charging times, 'shaming'
feed.summary: Apple's Clean Energy Charging feature, introduced in iOS 16.1, is causing a stir on social media among iPhone users. The feature aims to reduce carbon footprint by only charging devices when lower carbon-emission electricity is available, leading to some users experiencing slower charging times. Some users are critical, questioning the feature's environmental benefits and claiming Apple "shames" them when it's deactivated.

feed.title: Why Target says 2022 was rougher than 2020
feed.summary: Target Coo John Mulligan stated that 2022 was the most challenging operating environment of his career, even more so than 2020 during the height of the pandemic. Soaring inflation, supply chain issues, and changing consumer patterns led to significant drops in operating income and gross margins. In response, Target plans to cut $2 billion to $3 billion in costs, invest in customer-facing upgrades, expand owned brands and retail media, focus on price points and loyalty programs, and enhance convenience with expanded drive-up returns and store remodels.

feed.title: The 10 most innovative companies in augmented and virtual reality of 2023
feed.summary: Fast Company has released its list of the 10 most innovative companies in augmented and virtual reality for 2023, highlighting AR and VR advancements despite a decrease in consumer headset sales. The article discusses how companies like Unity, Meta, Google, Niantic, and Varjo are pushing the boundaries of spatial computing for both consumer and enterprise applications. It also notes Apple's secretive efforts in mixed reality and the overall steady improvement in hardware and software for spatial computing experiences.

feed.title: PNC Bank ends business relationship with Donald Trump Jr's MxM News app company 'without cause'
feed.summary: PNC Financial Services Group Inc. has terminated its business relationship with MxM News, Donald Trump Jr.'s news aggregation app company, without cause. MxM News co-founder Taylor Budowich stated that PNC cited terms and conditions allowing them to re-evaluate business relationships at any time. Trump Jr. criticized the move, calling it another example of "woke corporations" targeting conservatives, but confirmed the issue was quickly resolved for MxM News.

feed.title: VT vs ACWI ETF: Which is the Best World ETF?
feed.summary: This article compares two popular World ETFs, Vanguard's Total World Stock Index Fund ETF (VT) and iShares' MSCI ACWI ETF (ACWI), to help investors decide which is best for their portfolio. It delves into their differences in expense ratios, underlying indices, geographical breakdown, number of constituents, dividend yields, and fund managers, as well as their similarities in strategy, purpose, returns, liquidity, and sector breakdown. The author concludes that VT offers a lower expense ratio and exposure to smaller-cap companies, which might appeal to investors seeking that longer-tail exposure.

feed.title: IBM Stock: What Is the Highest Price It Has Ever Been?
feed.summary: IBM stock reached its all-time high of $148.74 on December 13, 2022, primarily driven by its acquisition of Red Hat and expansion into AI technology. Despite a recent decline and traditionally underperforming compared to some tech giants, its investments in hybrid cloud services and AI suggest potential for future growth, making it a possible long-term investment due to being undervalued.

feed.title: WEBTOON and LINE Studio Announce March 14 Launch Date for the True Beauty Mobile Puzzle Game
feed.summary: WEBTOON and LINE Studio have announced that the "True Beauty" mobile puzzle game will launch globally on March 14, available on Google Play and Apple App Store. The game is based on the popular webcomic "True Beauty," which has accumulated over six billion views, and will feature puzzle-style gameplay, fan-favorite characters, and customizable upgrades. Pre-registration for the game is currently open, offering limited edition in-game rewards.

feed.title: IOO: Some Reasons To Stay Faithful Despite The Long Fall Ahead (NYSEARCA:IOO)
feed.summary: The iShares Global 100 ETF (IOO) is an international equity ETF with a diversified portfolio, including major tech companies like Apple and Microsoft. Although the ETF may face a challenging medium term, the author believes it has long-term profit potential. Investors should consider its alternatives as economic recovery in the US could benefit IOO.

feed.title: British chip giant Arm chooses New York listing in a blow to London
feed.summary: British chip designer Arm, owned by Japan's SoftBank, and building materials group CRH have opted for U.S. stock market listings, delivering a blow to London's efforts to attract major companies. Arm stated that a U.S.-only listing in 2023 is the "best path forward," while CRH aims for increased commercial and acquisition opportunities in New York. This decision reinforces concerns that the U.K. stock market is struggling to compete with international rivals despite the British government's lobbying efforts and reforms to capital market rules.

feed.title: Apple Readies Its Next Range of Macs, Including — Finally — a New iMac
feed.summary: Apple is preparing to release a new lineup of Mac computers, notably including an updated iMac model. In addition to hardware news, the company is also facing changes in its executive team with the departure of its cloud chief, progressing with iOS and iPadOS updates, and recently secured an antitrust victory.

feed.title: TSMC customer billed NT$529bn
feed.summary: TSMC's largest customer, widely believed to be Apple Inc., generated NT$529.65 billion (US$17.32 billion) in revenue for the company last year, marking the first time a single customer contributed over NT$500 billion. Despite this record revenue, the customer's share of TSMC's total sales decreased from 26% in 2021 to 23% in 2022. This shift is attributed to significant growth in TSMC's automotive chip sales, which outperformed those for high-performance computing and smartphones.

feed.title: Sonos CEO Patrick Spence on right to repair, spatial audio and those never-ending lawsuits
feed.summary: Sonos CEO Patrick Spence discusses the company's new Era 100 and Era 300 speakers, focusing on the emergence of spatial audio and its mainstream adoption challenges. He also addresses Sonos' commitment to right-to-repair principles, its premium pricing strategy, the future of the smart home market, and ongoing lawsuits with Google.

feed.title: Virtual try-on is being hit by class actions. Should brands worry?
feed.summary: Fashion and beauty brands like Dior, Louis Vuitton, and Estée Lauder are facing class-action lawsuits over their use of biometric data collected through virtual try-on technology under the Illinois Biometric Information Privacy Act (BIPA). These lawsuits highlight the need for brands to prioritize clear disclosure and consent regarding customer data, particularly sensitive biometric information, even as virtual try-on continues to grow in popularity and benefits. While legal challenges may represent "teething problems" for the industry, experts emphasize the importance of robust security and transparency practices to navigate evolving privacy regulations.

feed.title: Apple and Foxconn efforts win labour reforms to advance Indian production plans - FT
feed.summary: Apple and its supplier Foxconn successfully lobbied for significant labor law reforms in India's southern state of Karnataka. These new laws permit 12-hour shifts and night work for women, aligning with practices in China. This move is part of Apple's broader strategy to diversify its production away from China due to COVID-19 disruptions and geopolitical tensions, with new iPhone assembly plants planned for Karnataka.

feed.title: Sonos Bets on Spatial Audio as a Key Brand Differentiator
feed.summary: Sonos is positioning spatial audio as a critical differentiator with its new Era 100 and Era 300 speakers. The Era 300 is the first Sonos speaker to support spatial audio, delivering an immersive 3D audio experience tailored for movie and TV content, and is anticipated to be a definitive way to listen to spatial audio mixes. The company believes embracing spatial audio will solidify its leadership in the premium audio market, even as wider streaming support for the technology is still developing.

feed.title: Apple Inc. to Launch Apple Music Classical App
feed.summary: Apple Inc. is launching a new standalone app called Apple Music Classical, specifically designed for classical music enthusiasts. This app will feature over 5 million tracks, immersive spatial audio, curated playlists, composer biographies, and complete metadata for accurate displays. It will be available for pre-order and officially launch on March 28, 2023, at no extra cost for existing Apple Music subscribers.

feed.title: Four Ways to Cluster based on Correlation
feed.summary: This article explores four methods to cluster 20 companies based on the correlation of their stock prices. It aims to see if these methods can accurately classify companies into categories like tech or oil and gas, as an alternative to clustering based on raw stock prices. The objective is to validate whether correlation-based clustering reveals meaningful industry groupings.

feed.title: Super-investor Mohnish Pabrai’s value investing principles
feed.summary: Mohnish Pabrai, founder and CEO of Pabrai Investment Funds, is a value investor inspired by Warren Buffett. He has amassed a significant net worth by focusing on undervalued stocks for the long term, despite only holding a few select companies. The article outlines five of Pabrai's key investment strategies, including minimizing downside risk, avoiding shorting, approaching retail investments cautiously, buying at "maximum pessimism," and understanding a company's "DNA structure."

feed.title: From Apple to Boeing, India is being put to the test as China manufacturing alternative
feed.summary: India is emerging as a potential manufacturing alternative to China, attracting major companies like Boeing and Apple. While significant deals, such as Boeing's record-breaking aircraft order, signal growing ties, India faces substantial hurdles including bureaucracy, lagging infrastructure, and red tape in its ambition to become a global manufacturing hub. Despite these challenges, India's economic growth projections and strategic partnerships indicate a strong push towards self-reliance and increased integration into global supply chains.

feed.title: Federal Circuit Allows Apple’s Fintiv Challenge To Proceed
feed.summary: The Federal Circuit Court of Appeals has revived Apple's challenge to the PTAB's Fintiv practice of discretionary denials, allowing it to proceed to the district court. This decision questions the USPTO's ability to issue rules through precedential decisions rather than traditional notice-and-comment rulemaking. The resolution of this case could significantly impact how the USPTO operates regarding Inter Partes Review practices.

feed.title: Fall Detection Systems Market Current Status and Future
feed.summary: The Fall Detection Systems market is experiencing significant growth driven by an aging global population and increased chronic diseases. These systems are crucial for quickly alerting caregivers or emergency services in the event of a fall, providing peace of mind for seniors and their families, and helping to maintain independence. The market is segmented by type, technology, and end-user, with key players like Koninklijke Philips N.V., ADT Security Services, and Apple Inc. involved in developing these essential health monitoring solutions.

feed.title: MLBN and Apple TV+ national broadcasts to show Zoom Replay Operations Center live under new deal
feed.summary: Major League Baseball (MLB) and Zoom Video Communications (NASDAQ: ZM) have announced a new partnership that will enhance the fan viewership experience by showing the Zoom Replay Operations Center live on MLB Network and Apple TV+ national broadcasts. This collaboration aims to create a more transparent replay review process, allowing fans to see the decision-making in action and hear from expert rules analysts. Beyond broadcasts, the deal also includes standardizing replay reviews with Zoom technology for on-field umpires and broader league-wide Zoom integration for secure information transmission, including the MLB Draft.

feed.title: Los avisos de consumo que implementó el BBVA en la app (incluye débito)
feed.summary: BBVA has implemented an immediate consumption alert system in its banking app, including for debit card transactions, which was not previously available through traditional card services like Visa Home or MasterConsultas. This new feature provides real-time notifications for purchases, improving security and enabling better tracking of expenses, especially for travelers. The author discusses the system's effectiveness, contrasting it with the outdated notifications from card networks, and hopes other banks will adopt similar immediate alert systems.

feed.title: Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway speeds up stock buybacks
feed.summary: Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway Inc. has significantly increased its stock buybacks, repurchasing over $1.8 billion worth of its shares this year. This move suggests that Buffett and Charlie Munger believe Berkshire's stock is undervalued, following nearly $60 billion in buybacks between 2020 and 2022. The company sees repurchases as a prudent use of its substantial cash reserves, despite a proposed quadrupling of the 1% excise tax on buybacks.
